title: Stage 4 Phase 1: Threshold non-binding (10-seed validation)

content.hypothesis: Plasticity threshold (0.01) is a binding structural parameter — varying it over [0.01, 0.1] (100x range) produces measurable MI gap differences, making it a viable Stage 4 adaptive target.
content.method: Two-phase test. Phase 1 (Task #10, stage4_diagnostic.py): sweep threshold over [0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.1], 3 seeds, K=6,8, n_perm=8, n_trials=6. Phase 2 (Task #11, stage4_threshold_validation.py): 10-seed validation of threshold=[0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.1], K=[4,6,8,10], n_perm=8, n_trials=6, with Mann-Whitney U and Cohen's d vs canonical.
content.result: FAILED. Phase 1 found apparent +20% improvement at threshold=0.1 (MI gap +0.0288). Phase 2 at 10 seeds showed no significant difference across all threshold values. Best candidate threshold=0.02 is only +3.0% with p=0.60. All conditions p > 0.5. The Phase 1 result was 3-seed noise confounded by CV=37% seed variance.
content.root_cause: The 3-seed Phase 1 diagnostic used independent seeds per threshold condition. With CV=37%, independent 3-seed samples cannot distinguish real effects from sampling noise. A paired-seed design would have caught this. The threshold result mirrors eta: the parameter is structurally real (regime selector) but performance is insensitive to its value across the tested range.
content.implication: Threshold, like eta, is vacuously non-binding. The plasticity rule is robust to threshold variation. Stage 4 must focus on remaining structural candidates: alpha_clip_lo and alpha_clip_hi (binding status unconfirmed, pending MI sweep).

constraints_implied: Plasticity threshold is non-binding — the system is insensitive to threshold variation across [0.01, 0.1]
Phase 1 diagnostics using 3 independent seeds per condition cannot detect effects reliably at CV=37% — always use paired seeds or 10+ seeds
